title: Integration tests conditionally skip when datasets absent
evidence: loader_test.go:31 t.Skipf("datasets not present under %s; skipping integration test", testDataDir)
suggestion: Acceptable pattern for integration tests; datasets are present in this archive so tests do execute

title: Test coverage at 70.3% — some code paths untested
evidence: scores.json test_coverage=0.703; 25 test functions across 4 files cover query engine and MCP layer but edge cases in loader/normalize may lack coverage
suggestion: Add tests for malformed CSV rows, empty datasets, and error paths in loader.go

title: Moderate maintainability score (0.54)
evidence: scores.json maintainability=0.5398; store.go (571 lines) and tools.go (488 lines) are large single files
suggestion: Consider splitting store.go query methods and tools.go handlers into separate files per capability area

title: Zero external dependencies — standard library only
evidence: go.mod has no require directives; no go.sum file; MCP protocol implemented from scratch in mcp.go
suggestion: Impressive minimalism; consider documenting this as a design choice

title: Cross-dataset deduplication for overlapping matches
evidence: store.go:169 MatchFilter.Dedup uses Match.signature() to collapse same game from multiple CSVs; standings use single-source priority (store.go:305)
suggestion: Good data-quality handling beyond spec requirements
